Manuscript DSP Code

31 March 2026

Overview of experiment and analyses

This is the complete analysis of the DSP task using white matter and bevaior and hormones.

Reading in and prepping our data

##### Mac Desktop path
working_dir <- "/Users/danielacossio/Desktop/Scripts/Projects/SNAG/DSI_studio/DSP"

# Moving to save all figrues and output into a different folder bc it's too big for github

outputdir <- "/Users/danielacossio/Documents/Chrastil_Lab/Projects/SNAG/DSI/DSP"

# path to the subject sheets 
midlife_DSP_df <- read.csv(paste0(working_dir,"/Mid_DSP_DSI.csv"))

yng_DSP_df <-  read.csv(paste0(working_dir,"/DSP_yng_participant_info.csv"))

yng_DSP_scan_df <-  read.csv(paste0(working_dir,"/DSP_yng_participant_info.csv")) %>%  filter(Subject_ID != 84) %>% filter(Subject_ID != 80)

Methods

Participants

Midlife

  • Total number of scans = 120 (males and females)

  • Total scans used in analysis= 69

  • Excluded subs 329 and 372 from analyses due to bad behavior data and Subject 347 and 424 failed quality control check in DSI

    • All subjects = 68
    • Men = 24
    • Women = 45
    • Pre = 18
    • Peri = 14
    • Post = 13 
midlife_DSP_df %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), min(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), max(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% `colnames<-`(c("N", "Mean age", "Age SD","Min age", "Max age")) %>%  kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
N Mean age Age SD Min age Max age
68 50.36765 3.745263 44 61 


midlife_DSP_df %>% group_by(sex) %>%  dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), min(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), max(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% `colnames<-`(c("sex","N", "Mean age", "Age SD","Min age", "Max age")) %>%  kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
sex N Mean age Age SD Min age Max age
Female 45 50.33333 3.567530 44 61
Male 23 50.43478 4.154145 44 61



Young

  • total subject with DSP = 27

  • Total scans used in analysis= 25

  • Excluded subs 380 and 384 due to low quality outliar

    • All subjects = 27
    • Men =
    • Women = 
yng_DSP_df %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), min(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), max(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE))  %>% `colnames<-`(c("N", "Mean age", "Age SD","Min age", "Max age")) %>% kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
N Mean age Age SD Min age Max age
27 20.25926 2.654352 18 28 



yng_DSP_df %>% group_by(sex) %>%  dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), min(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE), max(age_spatial_years, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% `colnames<-`(c("sex", "N", "Mean age", "Age SD","Min age", "Max age")) %>% kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
sex N Mean age Age SD Min age Max age
Female 9 20.55556 2.833333 18 26
Male 18 20.11111 2.632129 18 28

Scan parameters

  • Locations from UCSB and UCI



QSIprep

  • Version used: 0.16.0RC3

  • The following parameters were used

    • dwi_denoise_window 5
    • hmc_model 3dSHORE
    • hmc-transform Rigid
    • shoreline_iters 2

DSI Studio

  • Version and tags used via singularity container: chen-2024-05-03

  • Parameters

    • T-threshold = 2.5
    • Length = 25
    • Permutation = 4,000
    • pruning 4
    • QA
    • Covariates: sex, age, and FNAME

Hormones and behavior

I am doing a mediation analysis to understand the relation ship between white matter, hormones, and performance on the dual solution paradigm.

From shuying’s data, DSP is associated with estradial. Increased solution index is associated with increased solution index.

MD is negatively associated with Solution Index such that increased MD in the fornix is associated with less shortcuts. Essentially lower white matter integrity in fornix is associated with lower performance.

Hypothesis for mediation is that greater estradiol is driving the relationship in which greater integrity in the fornix for greater solution index.

X= Mean diffusivity in fornix
Y= greater SI on DSP task
M= Estradiol


Subject Numbers for DSP analyses Total SNAG numbers: 137
* 60 removed Total participants with DSP: 77 Total participants with DSI scans: 73 Total participants used in analyses: 69

Shuyings numbers are as follows from her behavioral task, idk who she excluded:
Women: 47
MEn:28
Total:75

Daniela’s

Women: 45
Men: 23
Total: 68

Subjects I excluded 347 for Low quality outliar 424 for low quality outliar 372 for neurochistosomaisis 388 for cyst in cerebellum 352 for depression

These were excluded later from hippocampal analyses so i just excluded their behavior here as well. Adding these in gives me 77 which is still not the correct number of subjects. I’m not sure which one is the extra. For now I’ll add them back in but it shouldn’t affect much.

Results

Young Adult Summary Stats

Total number of young adults with DSP

  • N = 27
  • Mean SI = 0.27
  • SD SI = 0.15
yng_DSP_df %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), min(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), max(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% `colnames<-`(c("N", "Mean SI", "SI SD","Min SI", "Max SI"))%>% kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
N Mean SI SI SD Min SI Max SI
27 0.2681167 0.1542762 0.0526316 0.7


Total number of young adults with DSP+good scan

  • N = 25
  • Mean = 0.26
  • SD = 0.16
yng_DSP_scan_df %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), min(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), max(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE))%>% `colnames<-`(c("N", "Mean SI", "SI SD","Min SI", "Max SI")) %>% kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
N Mean SI SI SD Min SI Max SI
25 0.2607239 0.1571965 0.0526316 0.7


Midlife Adults Summary Stats

Total number of midlife adults

  • n = 68
  • Mean = 0.23
  • SD = 0.17
midlife_DSP_df %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), min(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), max(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% `colnames<-`(c( "N", "Mean SI", "SI SD","Min SI", "Max SI")) %>% kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
N Mean SI SI SD Min SI Max SI
68 0.2353573 0.1705766 0 0.8


Midlife stats grouped by sex

  • Men
    • n = 23
    • mean = 0.19
    • SD = 0.15


  • women
    • n = 45
    • mean = 0.25
    • SD = 0.18
group_by(midlife_DSP_df, sex) %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), min(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), max(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% `colnames<-`(c("sex", "N", "Mean SI", "SI SD","Min SI", "Max SI"))%>% kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
sex N Mean SI SI SD Min SI Max SI
Female 45 0.2580251 0.1778572 0 0.80
Male 23 0.1910073 0.1491239 0 0.55


Midlife stats grouped by REPO

  • Pre
    • n = 18
    • mean = 0.31
    • SD = 0.17
  • Peri Post
    • n = 27
    • mean = 0.22
    • SD = 0.18
group_by(midlife_DSP_df, repo_status) %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), min(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE), max(dsp_si_true_pct, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% `colnames<-`(c("REPO", "N", "Mean SI", "SI SD","Min SI", "Max SI")) %>% kableExtra::kable() %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options =  c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
REPO N Mean SI SI SD Min SI Max SI
Men 23 0.1910073 0.1491239 0.0000000 0.5500000
Peri 14 0.2589358 0.2072314 0.0000000 0.8000000
Post 13 0.1874334 0.1458982 0.0000000 0.5454545
Pre 18 0.3082997 0.1658939 0.0666667 0.8000000 
# combining peri and post into one group
midlife_DSP_df$repo_status[midlife_DSP_df$repo_status =="Peri"]<-c("Peri_Post")
midlife_DSP_df$repo_status[midlife_DSP_df$repo_status =="Post"]<-c("Peri_Post")


#Creating group DF
Mid_dsp_fem_df <- midlife_DSP_df %>% filter(sex == "Female")
Mid_dsp_male_df <- midlife_DSP_df %>% filter(sex == "Male")
Mid_dsp_pre_df <- midlife_DSP_df %>% filter(repo_status == "Pre")
Mid_dsp_peripost_df <- midlife_DSP_df %>% filter(repo_status != "Pre") %>% filter(repo_status != "Men")


Group comparisons

Midlife vs Young

Solution Index

Check normality of group

Midlife solution index is not normally distributed p < 0.001

shapiro.test(midlife_DSP_df$dsp_si_true_pct) 
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  midlife_DSP_df$dsp_si_true_pct
## W = 0.9189, p-value = 0.0002913


young solution index is not normally distributed p < 0.001

shapiro.test(yng_DSP_scan_df$dsp_si_true_pct)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  yng_DSP_scan_df$dsp_si_true_pct
## W = 0.92772, p-value = 0.07698


Since one is not normal, I will use wilconox test

Young adults do not take more shortcuts than midlife adults, although we see it with shuyings data W = 749.5 p-value = 0.3857

wilcox.test(midlife_DSP_df$dsp_si_true_pct, yng_DSP_scan_df$dsp_si_true_pct)
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  midlife_DSP_df$dsp_si_true_pct and yng_DSP_scan_df$dsp_si_true_pct
## W = 749.5, p-value = 0.3857
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0


Creating Graph

all_SI_df <- midlife_DSP_df %>% dplyr::select(c("subject_id","sex","age_group","age_scan_years","dsp_si_true_pct","dsp_success_pct"))

temp_yng_df <- yng_DSP_scan_df %>% dplyr::select(c("Subject_ID","sex","age_group","age_scan_years","dsp_si_true_pct","dsp_success_pct")) %>% `colnames<-`(c("subject_id","sex","age_group","age_scan_years","dsp_si_true_pct","dsp_success_pct"))

all_SI_df <- rbind(all_SI_df,temp_yng_df)
dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_SI_df %>% ggplot(aes(
  x = factor(age_group, levels = c("Young", "Midlife")),
  y = dsp_si_true_pct,
  fill = age_group
)) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5,
                  fatten = 1,
                  outliers = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.3, size = 2) +
  #geom_point(size=2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#7d4c5a", "#6c6c6e")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  xlab("Age Group") +
  ylab("Solution Index (%)") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1 , 1, 1, "cm"),
  )
## Warning: The `fatten` argument of `geom_boxplot()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 4.0.0.
## ℹ Please use the `median.linewidth` argument instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_age_diff_si.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image

Success

Young adults are significacnlty better at wayfinding than midlife adults
W = 223, p-value = 4.806e-08

wilcox.test(midlife_DSP_df$dsp_success_pct, yng_DSP_scan_df$dsp_success_pct)
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  midlife_DSP_df$dsp_success_pct and yng_DSP_scan_df$dsp_success_pct
## W = 223, p-value = 4.806e-08
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0


dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_SI_df %>% ggplot(aes(
  x = factor(age_group, levels = c("Young", "Midlife")),
  y = dsp_success_pct,
  fill = age_group
)) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5,
                  fatten = 1,
                  outliers = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.3, size = 2) +
  #geom_point(size=2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#7d4c5a", "#6c6c6e")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  xlab("Age Group") +
  ylab("DSP Success") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_age_diff_success.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image

Midlife men vs women

Not sig different here either’ W = 653.5, p-value = 0.07849

wilcox.test(Mid_dsp_fem_df$dsp_si_true_pct, Mid_dsp_male_df$dsp_si_true_pct)
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(Mid_dsp_fem_df$dsp_si_true_pct,

## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0

  fill = sex
)) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5,
                  fatten = 1,

  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  xlab("Sex") +
  ylab("Solution Index (%)") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_sexDiff.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image

Midlife X Young X sex and age interaction

Solution Index

Here we are using the “all_SI_df” variable which as mid and young adults

ANOVA

No main effect of age or sex but an interaction Relationship between SI and age is dependent on sex 0.0144

DSP_SI_ANOVA <- aov(dsp_si_true_pct ~ age_scan_years * sex, data = all_SI_df)
#There is a marginal effect of REPO on performance 

summary(DSP_SI_ANOVA)
##                    Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)  
## age_scan_years      1 0.0335 0.03348   1.266 0.2635  
## sex                 1 0.0027 0.00267   0.101 0.7512  
## age_scan_years:sex  1 0.1649 0.16486   6.235 0.0144 *
## Residuals          89 2.3533 0.02644                 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_SI_df %>% ggplot(aes(
  x = factor(age_group, levels = c("Young", "Midlife")),
  y = dsp_si_true_pct,
  fill = interaction(age_group,sex),
)) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5,
                  fatten = 1,
                  outliers = FALSE,
                    color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.3, size = 2) +
  #geom_point(size=2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Young.Male ="#2d2d2d", Young.Female ="#3e262d", Midlife.Female = "#7d4c5a", Midlife.Male = "#6c6c6e")) + 
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  xlab("Age Group") +
  ylab("Solution Index") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_SI_age_sex_boxplot.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image
# see code below to add a significance label
 #ggsignif::geom_signif(annotations ="*", y_position = c(1), xmin = c(2), xmax =c(3)) +


Success

effect of age <0.0001 effect of sex = 0.0121

no interaction

DSP_success_ANOVA <- aov(dsp_success_pct ~ age_scan_years * sex, data = all_SI_df)
#There is a marginal effect of REPO on performance 

summary(DSP_success_ANOVA)
##                    Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
## age_scan_years      1 0.9193  0.9193  41.548 5.77e-09 ***
## sex                 1 0.1450  0.1450   6.555   0.0121 *  
## age_scan_years:sex  1 0.0002  0.0002   0.008   0.9280    
## Residuals          89 1.9692  0.0221                     
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_SI_df %>% ggplot(aes(
  x = factor(age_group, levels = c("Young", "Midlife")),
  y = dsp_success_pct,
  fill = interaction(age_group,sex),
)) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5,
                  fatten = 1,
                  outliers = FALSE,
                    color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.3, size = 2) +
  ggsignif::geom_signif(annotations ="*", y_position = c(1.1), xmin = c(0.7), xmax =c(2.25)) +
  #geom_point(size=2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Young.Male ="#2d2d2d", Young.Female ="#3e262d", Midlife.Female = "#7d4c5a", Midlife.Male = "#6c6c6e")) + 
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  xlab("Age Group") +
  ylab("Solution Index") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_success_age_s_boxplot.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image
# see code below to add a significance label
 #+

Midlife pre vs peri_post

SI

These are slighlty significantly different 0.037

wilcox.test(Mid_dsp_pre_df$dsp_si_true_pct, Mid_dsp_peripost_df$dsp_si_true_pct)
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(Mid_dsp_pre_df$dsp_si_true_pct,
## Mid_dsp_peripost_df$dsp_si_true_pct): cannot compute exact p-value with ties
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  Mid_dsp_pre_df$dsp_si_true_pct and Mid_dsp_peripost_df$dsp_si_true_pct
## W = 333, p-value = 0.03783
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0
all_menopause_df <- rbind(Mid_dsp_pre_df,Mid_dsp_peripost_df)
dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_menopause_df %>% ggplot(aes(
  x = factor(repo_status, levels = c("Pre", "Peri_Post")),
  y = dsp_si_true_pct,
  fill = repo_status
)) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5,
                  fatten = 1,
                  outliers = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.3, size = 2) +
  #geom_point(size=2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#7d4c5a", "#6c6c6e")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  xlab("REPO status") +
  ylab("Solution Index (%)") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_meno_diff_SI.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image


Success

not significantly different 0.3645

wilcox.test(Mid_dsp_pre_df$dsp_success_pct, Mid_dsp_peripost_df$dsp_success_pct)
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(Mid_dsp_pre_df$dsp_success_pct,
## Mid_dsp_peripost_df$dsp_success_pct): cannot compute exact p-value with ties
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  Mid_dsp_pre_df$dsp_success_pct and Mid_dsp_peripost_df$dsp_success_pct
## W = 282.5, p-value = 0.3645
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0
dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_menopause_df %>% ggplot(aes(
  x = factor(repo_status, levels = c("Pre", "Peri_Post")),
  y = dsp_success_pct,
  fill = repo_status
)) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5,
                  fatten = 1,
                  outliers = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.3, size = 2) +
  #geom_point(size=2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#7d4c5a", "#6c6c6e")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  xlab("REPO status") +
  ylab("Success (%)") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_meno_diff_success.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image

Group hormone comparisons

hormone_comparison <-  data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = 0)) %>% `colnames<-`(c("hormone","statistic","p.value","method","alternative"))

Estradiol

Comparing pre and peri post hormone levels between pre and peri_post

Significantly different P < 0.001

est_wilx_test <- wilcox.test(Mid_dsp_pre_df$estradiol_spatial_pg_ml, Mid_dsp_peripost_df$estradiol_spatial_pg_ml) %>% broom::tidy() %>%  mutate(hormone = "Estradiol") %>% dplyr::select("hormone",everything())

hormone_comparison <- rbind(hormone_comparison, est_wilx_test)

Mid_dsp_pre_df %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(estradiol_spatial_pg_ml, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(estradiol_spatial_pg_ml, na.rm = TRUE))
##   count     mean       sd
## 1    18 113.2072 90.81308
Mid_dsp_peripost_df %>% dplyr::summarise(count = n(), mean = mean(estradiol_spatial_pg_ml, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(estradiol_spatial_pg_ml, na.rm = TRUE))
##   count     mean       sd
## 1    27 27.96667 45.15553
# Extract 9 x 6 portrait

dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_menopause_df %>% ggplot(aes(x = factor(repo_status, levels=c("Pre", "Peri_Post")), y = estradiol_spatial_pg_ml, fill= repo_status)) +
  geom_boxplot(width=0.5,fatten = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(shape=16) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#7d4c5a", "#CD8A9B"))+
  #scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
   xlab("Repo Status")+
   ylab("Estradiol") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -3, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 3, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 1,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_Est_boxplot.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image
Estradiol and SI

Partial correlations One-tailed correlation controlling for AGE using all females

Here we want to control for age. ] Originally shuying did one tailed but we decided to go with two tailed. Estradiol is significantly associated with SI when controlling for age (n=45, r(43)=0.37, p=0.014).

pcor.test(Mid_dsp_fem_df$estradiol_spatial_pg_ml,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$dsp_si_true_pct,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$age_spatial_years, 
          method = "spearman")
##   estimate    p.value statistic  n gp   Method
## 1 0.369274 0.01363063  2.575182 45  1 spearman

Estradiol is significantly associated with SI (n=45, r(43)=0.46, p=0.0016).

cor.test(all_menopause_df$estradiol_spatial_pg_ml,
          all_menopause_df$dsp_si_true_pct,
          method = "spearman") 
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  all_menopause_df$estradiol_spatial_pg_ml and all_menopause_df$dsp_si_true_pct
## S = 8235.5, p-value = 0.00158
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4574789

SCatter plot

# Using 
#PDF 9 X 6

dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_menopause_df %>% ggplot(aes(x = estradiol_spatial_pg_ml,y = dsp_si_true_pct, color=repo_status)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = factor(repo_status)), size=3) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c( "#7d4c5a","#CD8A9B")) + 
   stat_smooth(method = "lm",
              formula = y ~ x,
              geom = "smooth",
             color="Black",
             se=FALSE)+
  xlab("Estradiol (pg/mL)")+
  ylab("Solution Index (%)") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -4, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      vjust = -2,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",
      hjust = 1,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "black",
                                linewidth =1 ),
    #axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(1,1, 1, 1, "cm"),
  )

ggsave(paste0(outputdir,"/DSP_Est_SI_scatterplot.tiff"))
## Saving 7 x 5 in image
Estradiol and Success

Partial correlations Originally shuying did one tailed but we decided to go with two tailed. Estradiol is significantly associated with SI when controlling for age (n=45, r(43)=0.37, p=0.014).

pcor.test(Mid_dsp_fem_df$estradiol_spatial_pg_ml,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$dsp_success_pct,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$age_spatial_years, 
          method = "spearman")

Estradiol is significantly associated with SI (n=45, r(43)=0.46, p=0.0016).

          all_menopause_df$dsp_success_pct,

  geom_point(aes(color = factor(repo_status)),size=3,alpha = 0.7) +

   geom_line(stat = "smooth", method = "lm",
            formula = y ~ x,
             alpha = 0.7,

            size = 1,
             se=FALSE) +

FSH + SI

Partial correlations One-tailed correlation controlling for AGE using all females

Here we want to control for age. ] Originally shuying did one tailed but we decided to go with two tailed. FSH is not significantly associated with SI when controlling for age (n=45, r(43)=-0.16, p=0.29).

pcor.test(Mid_dsp_fem_df$fsh_spatial_miu_ml,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$dsp_si_true_pct,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$age_spatial_years, 
          method = "spearman")
##     estimate  p.value statistic  n gp   Method
## 1 -0.1612583 0.295683 -1.058932 45  1 spearman

FSH is significantly associated with SI (n=45, r(43)=-0.32, p=0.03).

cor.test(all_menopause_df$fsh_spatial_miu_ml,
          all_menopause_df$dsp_si_true_pct,
          method = "spearman") 
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  all_menopause_df$fsh_spatial_miu_ml and all_menopause_df$dsp_si_true_pct
## S = 20012, p-value = 0.03308
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##        rho 
## -0.3183351

SCatter plot

# Using 
#PDF 9 X 6

dev.size()
## [1] 7 5
all_menopause_df %>% ggplot(aes(x = fsh_spatial_miu_ml,y = dsp_si_true_pct, color=repo_status)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = factor(repo_status)), size=3) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c( "#7d4c5a","#CD8A9B")) + 
   stat_smooth(method = "lm",
              formula = y ~ x,
              geom = "smooth",
             color="Black",
             se=FALSE)+
  xlab("FSH (pg/mL)")+
  ylab("Solution Index (%)") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -4, size = 24, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(vjust = 4, size = 24,face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      color = "black",

      color = "black",
      hjust = 1,

                                linewidth =1 ),

    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1),

  )

FSH + Success

Partial correlations One-tailed correlation controlling for AGE using all females

Here we want to control for age. ] Originally shuying did one tailed but we decided to go with two tailed. FSH is not significantly associated with SI when controlling for age (n=45, r(43)=0.002, p=0.99).

pcor.test(Mid_dsp_fem_df$fsh_spatial_miu_ml,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$dsp_success_pct,
          Mid_dsp_fem_df$age_spatial_years, 
          method = "spearman")
##      estimate   p.value  statistic  n gp   Method
## 1 0.001832835 0.9905791 0.01187815 45  1 spearman

FSH is significantly associated with success (n=45, r(43)=-0.07, p=0.66).

          all_menopause_df$dsp_success_pct,
          method = "spearman")

##

##         rho
